title: taraxacum ceratolobum
intro: Taraxacum ceratolobum, a lesser-known species of the dandelion genus, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Tibet and the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, Taraxacum ceratolobum is typically consumed as a tea or decoction, and its roots are often used in traditional Tibetan medicine to make medicinal compounds.
